description: This figure presents 3D cellular automaton (CA) simulation results showing the evolution of microstructure and dislocation density distribution on the YZ surface for low-alloy steel (0.398C-0.87Mn-0.31Si) during hot deformation at 950 °C. Panel (a) displays the transiently decreasing strain rate condition (from 0.1 s⁻¹ to 0.01 s⁻¹ at strain 0.4), with three rows representing strains of 0.4, 0.42, and 0.45, where DRX fractions progressively increase from 42.9% to 85.8% and finally to 96.7%. Panel (b) shows the constant strain rate condition (0.01 s⁻¹) at strain 0.42 with 83.0% DRX fraction. The microstructure maps use color coding: white grains represent the unrecrystallized matrix, green grains denote first-stage dynamic recrystallization (DRX), and red grains indicate second-stage DRX. The accompanying dislocation density maps reveal that high dislocation densities (up to 8×10¹³ m⁻², shown in dark red) initially concentrate in unrecrystallized regions (zone A) but decrease significantly and become more homogeneous as DRX progresses, dropping to approximately 0.01–1.8×10¹³ m⁻² in fully recrystallized areas. The comparison demonstrates that the transiently decreasing strain rate condition promotes faster DRX kinetics and more uniform dislocation density distribution compared to the constant low strain rate condition at equivalent strains.
